core_mechanism: A supercritical symmetry-breaking bifurcation (continuous phase transition) at which the emergent ordered pattern is set by the smoothest low-lying eigenmodes of a graph Laplacian defined on a similarity/'confusion' graph, so that the graph's topology fixes both the transition and an upper bound (of graph-coloring type) on the number of distinguishable states.
brief_reason: The abstract states the code emerges 'at a supercritical transition' where 'a small expansion is valid' and the outcome is 'governed by smooth modes of the Laplacian of errors,' whose 'topology of the error-graph' sets a map-coloring-related bound on the number of amino-acids.
